calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-05-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jessica Farinas appeared by telephone for Petitioner.

The Court questions the amount of fees requested for visits and phone calls in excess of what the last order found to be reasonable. Ms. Farinas stated that reimbursement to the caregiver/sister was for personal items (food, clothing, haircuts) that she had purchased and for mileage, that the Beneficiary had extensive needs, spoke of the Beneficiaries medical condition and stated that these medical issues/behavior required more visits, more involvement from the caregiver and more calls. Ms. Farina also stated that they were not paying hourly for the trips and that they were only paying for mileage. The Court informed Counsel that bond will be exonerated only after discharge. The Second and Final Account and Report of Successor Directed Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Mar-13-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Urbatsh, for and with petitioner Aida McGeachin.

Mr. Urbatsch reported that the first accouting was filed on Friday. Regarding the petition for disbursement of trust funds, the Court observed that the amount of time in providing caregiver services was excessive, particularly in the light of the requested fee of $45.00 per hour. Ms. McGeachin stated that her brother needed a lot of attention and assistance to participate in family gatherings and other activities. The Court noted that at the requested rate the trust estate would be quickly depleted, and stated that he would be reducing the compensation rate to $25 per hour, with a trimming of compensated time as appropriate. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

The Petition for Order Authorizing Disbursment of Trust Funds, etc. is continued to March 13, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Urbatsch to file a detailed declaration addressing all of the questions raised in the Probate Examiner's notes. ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-29-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lauren Trenor for (Petitioner) Fremont Bank.

Ms. Trenor addressed the issue of the annuity not being paid stating that neither her firm nor the trustee noticed that it was not being paid, that Pacific Life sent notice to the bank instead of the trustee, that on 4/24/15 Pacific Life sent a lump sum payment of $40,000.00 to Wells Fargo but it was in the name of the trustee, that on 5/17/15 the trustee sent a letter to Pacific Life indicating that he is the trustee, that payments are not being made at this time and that the matter is currently in the hands of the legal department of Pacific Life. The court stated that it may be appropriate to consider a penalty or surcharge. Ms. Trenor addressed the issue of the money being spent to prepare the accounting stating that because it was a pooled trust they had to hire someone to prepare the court accounting, clarified that the amount charged for the accounting was $877.50 not $1755.00 and that the pooled trust was necessary as he is over 65 years of age. The Accounting Petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
